text.title: Canary Go/No-Go Threshold Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Canary Go/No-Go Threshold Template` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Promote / rollback thresholds MUST be expressed relative to the prior stable release's measured baseline, not an absolute number.
rule.rationale: Absolute thresholds drift as the service grows; relative thresholds stay valid across traffic ramps.

rule.statement: Decision MUST evaluate ≥ 3 signals: latency (p95), error-rate, business-KPI (e.g. checkout-success). Single-signal promotion is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-signal canaries miss subtle regressions visible only in the business funnel.

rule.statement: Promotion only proceeds after the canary served ≥ 1000 user requests AND ≥ 5 minutes; whichever is later.
rule.rationale: Promoting on tiny traffic samples means the canary signal is noise.

rule.statement: Auto-rollback MUST trigger when any monitored signal breaches its threshold; manual override requires a named human + ADR entry.
rule.rationale: Manual rollback hesitation is the single biggest contributor to long incidents.

rule.statement: Each service's threshold sheet MUST list a named human owner who reviews the thresholds on every major release.
rule.rationale: Anonymous thresholds rot; named owner forces accountability per release.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
